content: Treating general-purpose computer agents as embodied ReAct-Reflexion reasoners that iteratively interact with the operating system environment (act) and verbalize their step-by-step reasoning (reflect) to correct off-target actions creates a transparent, self-correcting workflow for open-ended computer tasks like automated software debugging or multi-step document processing. This design eliminates the black box of end-to-end computer agent decision-making by grounding every action in explicit, reviewable reasoning that can be refined over repeated reflection cycles.
source_topics: agent planning reasoning frameworks ReAct Reflexion
trigger_topic: agent planning reasoning frameworks ReAct Reflexion
